products.name: Blueberries
products.hs_description: Cranberries, blueberries and other fruits of the genus Vaccinium, fresh
products.duty_notes: MFN duty listed as Free under Canada Customs Tariff Chapter 8. If frozen, classify under 0811 instead.

permits_and_requirements.notes: Confirm requirement using CFIA AIRS tool based on country of origin. Fresh fruit imports subject to plant health rules.
Expect possible inspection on arrival. Product must be clean and pest-free.
Use CFIA AIRS (Automated Import Reference System) to confirm exact import conditions for specific origin country.

products.systems_approach: CFIA plant health rules apply to fresh blueberry imports. Exact requirements depend on origin country — confirm via AIRS.

products.name: Asparagus
products.duty_notes: Many asparagus lines show MFN duty as Free. Classification depends on whether product is for processing or other use.

permits_and_requirements.notes: Asparagus treated as plant product subject to plant health requirements. Confirm via AIRS.
Product must be fresh or chilled (not frozen or canned), clean and pest-free.
Use AIRS to confirm origin-specific requirements and documents needed.

products.systems_approach: Asparagus falls under CFIA plant health requirements. Verify fresh vs chilled classification and origin conditions via AIRS.

products.name: Grapes
products.duty_notes: Many grape lines show MFN duty as Free. Exact tariff line depends on product description in tariff schedule.

permits_and_requirements.notes: CFIA requirements vary by origin and pest risk. Confirm via AIRS for specific country.
Must be fresh table grapes (not dried raisins). Prepare for inspection if required.
CFIA temperate fresh fruit framework applies. Use AIRS to confirm shipment requirements.

products.systems_approach: CFIA has a temperate fresh fruit framework (D-95-08). AIRS is the official tool to confirm shipment requirements by origin.

products.name: Coffee (Green / Unroasted)
products.duty_notes: Green coffee enters Canada duty-free under MFN rate. Confirm via CBSA Chapter 9.

permits_and_requirements.notes: CFIA requirements are less complex for coffee than fresh produce. Check AIRS if product has additives or is flavored.

products.systems_approach: Not applicable for standard green coffee. No CFIA plant health permit typically required.

products.name: Coffee (Roasted)
products.duty_notes: Roasted coffee enters Canada duty-free under MFN rate. Confirm via CBSA Chapter 9.

permits_and_requirements.notes: Check AIRS if product has additives or flavoring. Standard roasted coffee has minimal CFIA requirements.

products.systems_approach: Not applicable. Roasting eliminates plant health concerns.

products.name: Quinoa
products.duty_notes: Quinoa is specifically listed under HS 1008.50 in Canada Customs Tariff. MFN duty is Free.

permits_and_requirements.notes: Use AIRS to confirm grain import conditions by origin country. Food grain is treated differently from seed for planting.
CFIA seed import rules only apply if quinoa is being imported for propagation purposes, not for human consumption.

products.systems_approach: Food grain quinoa — CFIA seed rules do not apply. State clearly that product is for human consumption in import documentation.

universal_compliance_notes: Always use CFIA AIRS to confirm import requirements for specific origin country
CBSA Customs Tariff is the official source for HS codes and MFN duty rates in Canada
CFIA regulates fresh produce under plant health directives
Requirements vary significantly by country of origin
Phytosanitary certificates must be issued by the national plant protection organization of the exporting country
Food grain and seed for planting have different regulatory pathways under CFIA
